title: Real-Time Server-Sent Events
description:
Build a real-time notification dashboard that consumes a Server-Sent Events (SSE) stream, with connection management and reconnection logic.

### Requirements

- Connect to an SSE endpoint on a "Connect" button click
- Display incoming events in a scrollable list (newest first)
- Show connection status: disconnected, connecting, connected
- Handle named event types: `notification`, `alert`, `heartbeat`
- Style different event types differently
- Implement manual disconnect button
- Auto-reconnect on connection loss with configurable delay
- Show count of events received and time since connected
- Filter events by type with toggle checkboxes

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Connect button | `connect-btn` |
| Disconnect button | `disconnect-btn` |
| Status indicator | `connection-status` |
| Event list | `event-list` |
| Event item | `event-{index}` |
| Event count | `event-count` |
| Filter checkbox | `filter-{type}` |
| Connection time | `connection-time` |
constraints:
- Must use EventSource API
- Must handle onopen, onmessage, onerror lifecycle
- Must support named events via addEventListener
- Must clean up EventSource on disconnect/unmount
- Must auto-reconnect on error
- Events must be filtered client-side (not by unsubscribing)
hints: Create the EventSource with `new EventSource(url)`. Listen for named events: `source.addEventListener('notification', handler)` etc.
For auto-reconnect: in the `onerror` handler, close the source and set a timeout to reconnect. Use a ref to track the reconnect timer.
Keep all events in state but use the filter checkboxes to control which types are displayed. This way you don't lose events when toggling filters.
